backlog.title: Chrome-disable for every provider (FR-C1–C5, G25)
backlog.description: v2.9 delta. Make every built-in provider render chrome-less (skills, extensions/prompt-templates, context files, MCP servers) for every surface the agent CLI exposes a disable switch for, and treat any surface a provider cannot switch off as a documented, tracked limitation with a re-check date. This is a verification + documentation discipline layered on already-shipped provider manifests — no commit/CAS/rescue/lock changes, no mutation-safety change, no manifest schema change, no new CLI flags/env/config keys.

milestones.title: Manifest verification, CHROME-DISABLE notes, and tests
milestones.description: Audit each of the 7 built-in providers' bare_flags against captured --help findings for chrome-disable surfaces. Add CHROME-DISABLE notes to each provider's doc-comment block. Add test assertions pinning the chrome-disable contract. Mirror notes in providers/*.toml reference files.

tasks.title: Per-provider chrome audit + CHROME-DISABLE manifest notes
tasks.description: For each of the 7 built-in providers in internal/provider/builtin.go, verify which chrome surfaces {skills, extensions/prompt-templates, context files, MCP} the agent CLI exposes a disable switch for (against captured --help findings), set any exposed-but-unset chrome-disable flag in bare_flags (FR-C2), document the pi MCP gap (FR-C3), and add a CHROME-DISABLE note recording per surface what is disabled, what is not, and the verification date/source (FR-C5). Also mirror the notes in providers/*.toml reference files.

subtasks.title: Add CHROME-DISABLE notes to all 7 built-in providers in builtin.go
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/016_e6bf7715e06e/architecture/external_deps.md for the per-provider chrome surface inventory and plan/016_e6bf7715e06e/architecture/system_context.md for the full codebase state. The key finding: pi and claude already set every chrome-disable flag their CLIs expose; the 5 read-only-constrained providers (agy, qwen-code, opencode, codex, cursor) mostly expose no per-surface chrome switches. The only real gap is pi's lack of a --no-mcp flag (FR-C3).
2. INPUT: The 7 provider functions in internal/provider/builtin.go: builtinPi(), builtinClaude(), builtinAgy(), builtinQwenCode(), builtinOpenCode(), builtinCodex(), builtinCursor(). Each has an existing doc-comment block with TOOLS-DISABLE discussions and verification notes. The Manifest struct (internal/provider/manifest.go) has 22 fields — no ChromeDisable field exists; chrome-disable is expressed via existing bare_flags plus doc-comment notes.
3. LOGIC: For each provider, add a CHROME-DISABLE note paragraph to its doc-comment block (FR-C5). The note must record, per chrome surface {skills, extensions/prompt-templates, context files, MCP}: what is disabled (by which flag), what is not (no switch available), and the verification date/source — the same discipline as the existing FR-D5/FR-T9 verification notes already in those headers.
   - **pi** (§12.3): Note that --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, --no-context-files disable four chrome surfaces (already in bare_flags). Document the MCP gap (FR-C3): pi has NO --no-mcp flag (only --mcp-config <path>); --no-tools suppresses MCP tool *use* but configured servers may still be discovered/connected at startup — this is a tracked limitation, not an assumption.
   - **claude** (§12.4): Note that --tools "" disables all tools (MCP surfaces as tools) and --setting-sources "" blocks the settings files where MCP/skills/extensions are configured (both already in bare_flags). Chrome is covered.
   - **agy** (§12.5.1): Note that agy exposes no per-surface chrome-disable switches; --mode plan is the read-only constraint (mutation safety, not chrome). Chrome may load; the call stays read-only and never-mutate — documented limitation per FR-C4. Verification source: agy v1.1.0 --help, 2026-07-08.
   - **qwen-code** (§12.5.2): Same pattern as agy — no per-surface chrome switches; --approval-mode default is the read-only constraint. Documented limitation. Experimental (# TO CONFIRM).
   - **opencode** (§12.6): Same pattern — run subcommand is inherently read-only; no per-surface chrome switches on run. Documented limitation.
   - **codex** (§12.7): Same pattern — --sandbox read-only + --ephemeral are the read-only constraint; no per-surface chrome switches on exec. Documented limitation. Verified 2026-07-08 (codex-cli 0.143.0).
   - **cursor** (§12.7): Same pattern — --mode ask is the read-only constraint; no per-surface chrome switches. Documented limitation.
   VERIFY against plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/external_deps.md (the --help catalog) whether any of the 5 read-only-constrained providers actually DO expose a chrome-disable switch that is currently unset in bare_flags (FR-C2). If so, add the flag. Expected: near-zero additions — research shows they mostly have no per-surface chrome switches. Do NOT invent flags (FR-C4 is explicit).
4. OUTPUT: Modified internal/provider/builtin.go with CHROME-DISABLE note paragraphs in all 7 provider doc-comment blocks. The Manifest struct values (bare_flags etc.) may be unchanged or have 0-2 small additions. Each CHROME-DISABLE note must be consumable by P1.M1.T2.S1 (tests assert the flags the note claims are present in bare_flags).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The CHROME-DISABLE note IS the per-provider documentation artifact — it lives in internal/provider/builtin.go alongside the code it describes. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

subtasks.title: Mirror CHROME-DISABLE notes in providers/*.toml reference files
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: providers/*.toml are human-readable REFERENCE DOCUMENTATION that mirror the compiled-in manifests in builtin.go byte-for-byte (modulo comments). They are NOT loaded at runtime. They exist for: (a) human reference, (b) as a copy-paste template for config overrides.
2. INPUT: The 7 files in providers/ directory: pi.toml, claude.toml, agy.toml, qwen-code.toml, opencode.toml, codex.toml, cursor.toml. Each has a header comment block describing the provider. The CHROME-DISABLE notes from P1.M1.T1.S1 (in builtin.go) are the source of truth.
3. LOGIC: For each of the 7 providers/*.toml files, add a CHROME-DISABLE comment paragraph to the header comment block, mirroring the note added to the corresponding builtin*() function in P1.M1.T1.S1. Keep it concise (1-4 lines per provider). The note must match the builtin.go note in substance: what chrome surfaces are disabled (by which flag), what is not (no switch), and the limitation status.
4. OUTPUT: Modified providers/{pi,claude,agy,qwen-code,opencode,codex,cursor}.toml with CHROME-DISABLE comment paragraphs. Content mirrors P1.M1.T1.S1.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The TOML files are themselves documentation artifacts. This rides WITH the work.

tasks.title: Tests asserting the chrome-disable contract
tasks.description: Add focused assertions in internal/provider/builtin_test.go that pin the chrome-disable contract where it is testable from the manifest struct (no real agent needed). Assert pi/claude chrome-disable flag presence, read-only-constrained provider constraint-flag presence, and a lightweight note/flag consistency check.

subtasks.title: Add chrome-disable contract assertions to builtin_test.go
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: internal/provider/builtin_test.go already has comprehensive tests following a clear pattern: assertStr(t, name, got, want) / assertNilStr(t, name, ptr) helpers, reflect.DeepEqual for slices, provider-specific test functions (TestBuiltinManifests_PiFields, _ClaudeFields, etc.). The existing tests DO assert bare_flags contents (e.g., TestBuiltinManifests_PiFields asserts the exact 6-element BareFlags slice). Follow this exact pattern.
2. INPUT: The Manifest values from internal/provider/builtin.go (post-P1.M1.T1.S1). The CHROME-DISABLE notes in each provider's doc-comment are the claims to verify. The test helpers assertStr, assertNilStr, and the renderArgs function are available.
3. LOGIC: Add a focused test (or tests) in builtin_test.go asserting the chrome-disable contract:
   - **pi**: BareFlags contains --no-extensions, --no-skills, --no-prompt-templates, --no-context-files (the four chrome surfaces pi exposes switches for). These are already asserted by TestBuiltinManifests_PiFields as part of the exact-slice check, but add a focused contains-check that survives future flag reordering.
   - **claude**: BareFlags contains --setting-sources (whose "" value blocks settings files where MCP/skills/extensions are configured) and --tools (whose "" value disables all tools incl. MCP).
   - **Read-only-constrained providers**: assert the existing read-only constraint flag is present: codex (--sandbox + read-only as adjacent pair, or --sandbox present); cursor (--mode + ask); agy (--mode + plan); qwen-code (--approval-mode); opencode (empty BareFlags by design — run is inherently read-only). This confirms FR-C4(b): the mutation-safety guarantee that 'stays' is in fact present.
   - **Note/flag consistency** (optional, lightweight): for pi and claude, verify that the flags their CHROME-DISABLE notes claim are disabled actually appear in BareFlags. A simple approach: define expected-flag sets per provider and assert all are present via a containsAll helper.
   These are cheap table-driven tests; they do not invoke any agent. Follow the existing test file's style and helpers.
4. OUTPUT: Modified internal/provider/builtin_test.go with new chrome-disable test function(s). All existing tests must continue to pass (go test ./internal/provider/ -count=1).
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. Tests are internal.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)
milestones.description: Changeset-level documentation task that depends on M1 (it records the verified manifest state). Extends docs/providers.md (Chrome-disable column + asymmetry section bullet), docs/how-it-works.md (safety paragraph), docs/README.md (capability index), and top-level README.md (brief chrome mention). Per SOW §5, this is the Mode B catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with stale overview docs.

tasks.title: Extend docs/providers.md + cross-cutting overview for chrome-disable
tasks.description: Add a 'Chrome-disable' column to the 7-provider table in docs/providers.md (FR-C5). Add the 'Chrome is a separate axis' bullet to the Tools-disable asymmetry section (§12.7.1 v2.9 extension). Extend the docs/how-it-works.md safety paragraph with a chrome sentence. Add a capability-index entry to docs/README.md. Add a brief chrome-less mention to top-level README.md where safety messaging exists.

subtasks.title: Add Chrome-disable column and asymmetry bullet to docs/providers.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: docs/providers.md has two relevant sections: '## The 7 built-in providers' (a 7-row table with columns: Provider, Delivery, Print flag, Model flag, Default model, System prompt flag, Tool-disable approach, Stager?) and '## Tools-disable asymmetry' (a section with two bullets: explicit switch vs read-only constraint). The table uses markdown pipe syntax. See plan/016_e6bf7715e06e/architecture/system_context.md for the exact current content.
2. INPUT: The CHROME-DISABLE notes from P1.M1.T1.S1 are the source of truth for per-provider chrome status.
3. LOGIC:
   (a) **7-provider table**: Add a new column 'Chrome-disable' after 'Tool-disable approach'. Per-provider values (one short phrase each):
     - pi: 'extensions/skills/templates/context off (--no-* flags); MCP use suppressed (servers may connect — tracked limitation)'
     - claude: 'via --tools "" + --setting-sources ""'
     - agy: 'no per-surface switch; read-only constraint only — documented limitation'
     - qwen-code: 'no per-surface switch; read-only constraint only — documented limitation'
     - opencode: 'no per-surface switch; read-only by design — documented limitation'
     - codex: 'no per-surface switch; read-only constraint only — documented limitation'
     - cursor: 'no per-surface switch; read-only constraint only — documented limitation'
   (b) **Tools-disable asymmetry section**: Add a third bullet/paragraph after the existing two bullets: 'Chrome is a separate axis — mutation safety says nothing about agent chrome (skills, extensions, context files, MCP servers). See the Chrome-disable column above and the CHROME-DISABLE notes in each provider manifest. Providers that expose a per-surface disable switch set it (pi, claude); providers that do not document the limitation honestly (codex, cursor, opencode, agy, qwen-code) — the call stays read-only and never-mutate regardless.'
4. OUTPUT: Modified docs/providers.md with Chrome-disable column and asymmetry extension.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the documentation task — it records the verified manifest state from M1.

subtasks.title: Extend docs/how-it-works.md safety paragraph, docs/README.md index, and README.md
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Three files need cross-cutting chrome mentions:
   - docs/how-it-works.md: has '### Safety invariant' paragraph at ~line 197: 'No provider mutates the repository (PRD §18.1). Every built-in manifest constrains the agent to a read-only mode…' — covers mutation safety only.
   - docs/README.md: has a '## Capability index' section with bullet entries per capability.
   - README.md: safety messaging embedded in feature descriptions (line 4 hero pitch: 'can never corrupt your repo'; line 66 feature table). No dedicated bare-mode/chrome bullet.
2. INPUT: The CHROME-DISABLE notes from P1.M1.T1.S1 and the docs/providers.md updates from P1.M2.T1.S1 provide the authoritative content.
3. LOGIC:
   (a) **docs/how-it-works.md (~line 197, '### Safety invariant' paragraph)**: Extend with one sentence after the existing mutation-safety text: 'Every provider also renders chrome-less — skills, extensions, context files, and MCP servers are disabled wherever the agent CLI exposes a switch for them (pi and claude today); surfaces a provider cannot switch off are documented as tracked limitations rather than hidden assumptions. See [providers.md](providers.md#tools-disable-asymmetry) for the per-provider chrome-disable details.'
   (b) **docs/README.md — Capability index**: Add a one-line entry: '- **Chrome-disable (v2.9)** → [providers.md#tools-disable-asymmetry](providers.md#tools-disable-asymmetry) — every provider renders chrome-less where the agent CLI allows it.'
   (c) **README.md**: If it carries a safety/bare-mode bullet (check the feature table at ~line 66 or the FAQ), add a concise mention that commit-message calls are chrome-less where the agent allows it (skills/extensions/context/MCP disabled). Keep it brief — this is a refinement of existing 'bare mode' messaging, not a new feature pitch. Do NOT add a new top-level section.
4. OUTPUT: Modified docs/how-it-works.md (1 sentence addition), docs/README.md (1 capability-index line), README.md (brief mention).
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the documentation task — it syncs cross-cutting overview docs with the M1 implementation.
